name: Policies for information security
justification: Information security policies are required to establish management direction and support for information security across all Acme systems and operations.

name: Information security roles and responsibilities
justification: Formal assignment of information security roles and responsibilities is required to ensure accountability for ISMS controls across all functions.

name: Segregation of duties
justification: Segregation of duties is required to reduce the risk of fraud and error in payment processing and system administration functions.

name: Management responsibilities
justification: Management must actively support information security to ensure the ISMS is effective and resourced appropriately.

name: Contact with authorities
justification: Acme must maintain contacts with relevant regulatory authorities (FCA, ICO, NCSC) for incident response and compliance obligations.

name: Contact with special interest groups
justification: Membership of financial sector ISACs and relevant security communities supports threat intelligence and best practice sharing.

name: Threat intelligence
justification: Threat intelligence is required to inform risk assessments and proactive defence given Acme's exposure as a payments processor.

name: Information security in project management
justification: Security requirements must be integrated into all technology projects including payment system upgrades and new product development.

name: Inventory of information and other associated assets
justification: An asset inventory is required for risk management and to understand the scope of information requiring protection.

name: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
justification: All staff and contractors must understand permitted and prohibited uses of Acme information assets and systems.

name: Return of assets
justification: Assets must be returned upon termination of employment or contract to prevent unauthorised retention of sensitive information.

name: Classification of information
justification: Information classification is required to ensure appropriate protection proportionate to sensitivity given the volume of personal and payment data processed.

name: Labelling of information
justification: Labelling supports correct handling of classified information and reduces the risk of inadvertent disclosure.

name: Information transfer
justification: Controls over information transfer are required to protect data in transit between Acme and customers, partners, and suppliers.

name: Access control
justification: Access control is fundamental to protecting payment processing systems and customer data from unauthorised access.

name: Identity management
justification: Formal identity management is required to ensure only authorised individuals hold accounts and that accounts are managed throughout their lifecycle.

name: Authentication information
justification: Strong authentication is required for all access to Acme systems, particularly payment processing and cloud infrastructure.

name: Access rights
justification: Formal management of access rights including provisioning, review, and revocation is required to enforce least privilege across all systems.

name: Information security in supplier relationships
justification: Acme relies on key suppliers including AWS, Stripe, and other third parties whose security practices directly impact Acme's risk profile.

name: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
justification: Contractual security requirements must be established with all suppliers processing Acme data or providing critical services.

name: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
justification: ICT supply chain risks including software dependencies and cloud service provider security must be managed systematically.

name: Monitoring review and change management of supplier services
justification: Ongoing monitoring of supplier security performance is required given reliance on AWS and payment processor integrations.

name: Information security for use of cloud services
justification: Acme's entire infrastructure is cloud-based (AWS eu-west-1); a formal cloud security policy and governance framework is essential.

name: Information security incident management planning and preparation
justification: Incident management planning is required to enable effective response to security incidents affecting payment systems and customer data.

name: Assessment and decision on information security events
justification: A structured process for assessing whether events constitute incidents is required to ensure appropriate and timely response.

name: Response to information security incidents
justification: Effective incident response procedures are required to contain and remediate incidents affecting payment systems and to meet GDPR breach notification obligations.

name: Learning from information security incidents
justification: Post-incident review and lessons learned are required to prevent recurrence and improve the ISMS continuously.

name: Collection of evidence
justification: Evidence collection procedures are required to support investigations and regulatory reporting following security incidents.

name: Information security during disruption
justification: Business continuity planning must incorporate information security requirements to maintain protection during disruptions.

name: ICT readiness for business continuity
justification: AWS infrastructure resilience must be tested and documented to ensure payment service continuity in the event of failures.

name: Legal statutory regulatory and contractual requirements
justification: Acme operates under FCA authorisation, UK GDPR, PCI DSS v4.0, and other regulatory obligations that impose specific information security requirements.

name: Intellectual property rights
justification: Acme develops proprietary payment software and uses third-party components; IPR controls are required to manage licensing compliance.

name: Protection of records
justification: ISMS records and business records must be protected against loss, destruction, falsification, and unauthorised access in accordance with regulatory requirements.

name: Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information
justification: Acme processes personal data of payment customers; privacy and PII protection controls are mandatory under UK GDPR.

name: Independent review of information security
justification: Independent review is required by ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 and is necessary to provide objective assurance of ISMS effectiveness.

name: Compliance with policies rules and standards for information security
justification: Regular compliance checking ensures that controls are operating as intended and deviations are identified and corrected promptly.

name: Documented operating procedures
justification: Documented procedures for key operational processes are required to ensure consistent and correct execution of security controls.

name: Screening
justification: Pre-employment screening of staff with access to payment systems and customer data is required to mitigate insider threat risk.

name: Terms and conditions of employment
justification: Employment contracts must include information security obligations and acceptable use requirements for all staff.

name: Information security awareness education and training
justification: All staff require regular security awareness training to understand their responsibilities and recognise threats such as phishing.

name: Disciplinary process
justification: A formal disciplinary process for information security violations deters policy breaches and provides a consistent response framework.

name: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
justification: Post-termination security obligations including confidentiality must be clearly defined and enforced.

name: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
justification: NDAs are required for all staff and contractors with access to confidential payment data and proprietary systems.

name: Remote working
justification: All Acme staff are remote workers; remote working security controls are essential to protect access to cloud systems and data.

name: Information security event reporting
justification: All staff must be able to report security events quickly; a clear reporting mechanism supports prompt detection and response.

name: Physical security perimeters
justification: No owned or leased premises; co-location provider manages physical security under their own ISO 27001 certification.

name: Physical entry
justification: No owned or leased premises; co-location provider manages physical security under their own ISO 27001 certification.

name: Securing offices rooms and facilities
justification: No owned or leased premises; co-location provider manages physical security under their own ISO 27001 certification.

name: Physical security monitoring
justification: No owned or leased premises; co-location provider manages physical security under their own ISO 27001 certification.

name: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
justification: Applicable to staff home working environments; home office security guidance is required for all remote workers.

name: Working in secure areas
justification: No owned or leased premises; co-location provider manages physical security under their own ISO 27001 certification.

name: Clear desk and clear screen
justification: Clear desk and screen lock requirements apply to all remote workers handling sensitive documents and data.

name: Equipment siting and protection
justification: Staff laptops and home networking equipment must be appropriately sited and protected against environmental threats.

name: Security of assets off-premises
justification: No owned or leased premises; co-location provider manages physical security under their own ISO 27001 certification.

name: Storage media
justification: No owned or leased premises; co-location provider manages physical security under their own ISO 27001 certification.

name: Supporting utilities
justification: AWS data centre utilities (power, cooling) are managed by AWS; Acme must confirm and document this in supplier agreements.

name: Cabling security
justification: Applicable to staff home network cabling; basic requirements included in remote working policy.

name: Equipment maintenance
justification: Staff laptops require maintenance and update management through MDM (Microsoft Intune).

name: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
justification: Any physical equipment (laptops, USB devices) must be securely disposed of to prevent data recovery.

name: User endpoint devices
justification: All staff use personal or company-assigned endpoints to access Acme cloud systems; endpoint security controls are essential.

name: Privileged access rights
justification: Privileged access to AWS and production systems carries significant risk and requires specific additional controls beyond standard access management.

name: Information access restriction
justification: Access to information must be restricted based on classification and need-to-know to protect payment and personal data.

name: Access to source code
justification: Payment system source code is a sensitive asset; access must be controlled and monitored.

name: Secure authentication
justification: Strong authentication mechanisms are required for all access to cloud systems and payment infrastructure.

name: Capacity management
justification: Payment processing capacity must be managed to ensure availability and performance SLAs are met.

name: Protection against malware
justification: Endpoints and servers require protection against malware to prevent data exfiltration and service disruption.

name: Management of technical vulnerabilities
justification: Systematic vulnerability management is required to identify and remediate weaknesses in payment systems and infrastructure.

name: Configuration management
justification: Formal configuration management is required to ensure systems are built and maintained to approved secure baselines.

name: Information deletion
justification: Secure deletion of information is required when no longer needed to comply with data minimisation principles under UK GDPR.

name: Data masking
justification: PAN data and other sensitive payment information must be masked in non-production environments and in logs.

name: Data leakage prevention
justification: DLP controls are required to detect and prevent unauthorised exfiltration of payment and personal data.

name: Information backup
justification: Regular backups of payment data, configuration, and ISMS records are required to support recovery from incidents.

name: Redundancy of information processing facilities
justification: Payment processing systems must be resilient to single points of failure; AWS multi-AZ architecture must be documented.

name: Logging
justification: Comprehensive logging of system, security, and application events is required for detection, investigation, and forensic purposes.

name: Monitoring activities
justification: Active monitoring of logs and security events is required to detect incidents in payment systems promptly.

name: Clock synchronisation
justification: Synchronised clocks across all systems are required for accurate log correlation and forensic investigation.

name: Use of privileged utility programs
justification: Privileged utility programs capable of bypassing controls must be restricted and their use controlled and monitored.

name: Installation of software on operational systems
justification: Unauthorised software installation on production systems could introduce vulnerabilities or malware; a formal approval process is required.

name: Networks security
justification: Network security controls including firewalls, security groups, and network segmentation are required to protect payment systems.

name: Security of network services
justification: Network services used in the delivery of payment processing must be secured and their security characteristics understood.

name: Segregation of networks
justification: Segregation of the cardholder data environment from other networks is required by both ISO 27001 and PCI DSS.

name: Web filtering
justification: Web filtering is required to protect staff from malicious websites and to enforce acceptable use policy.

name: Use of cryptography
justification: Encryption of payment data in transit and at rest is mandatory under PCI DSS and UK GDPR; a cryptographic policy is required.

name: Secure development life cycle
justification: Payment processing software is developed in-house; a formal secure development lifecycle is essential to manage security risk in the development process.

name: Application security requirements
justification: Security requirements must be defined for all payment processing applications to ensure security is designed in from the outset.

name: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
justification: Secure architecture principles must be applied to payment system design to reduce attack surface and enforce defence in depth.

name: Secure coding
justification: Secure coding standards are required to prevent common vulnerabilities (OWASP Top 10) in payment processing software.

name: Security testing in development and acceptance
justification: Security testing must be integrated into the development pipeline and acceptance criteria for all payment system releases.

name: Outsourced development
justification: Any outsourced development of payment components must be subject to the same security requirements as in-house development.

name: Separation of development testing and production environments
justification: Separation of environments is required to prevent development and test activities from affecting production payment systems.

name: Change management
justification: All changes to payment systems and infrastructure must be managed through a formal change management process.

name: Test information
justification: Production payment data must not be used in test environments; data masking or synthetic data generation is required.

name: Protection of information systems during audit testing
justification: Audit and testing activities must be planned and controlled to avoid disruption or damage to production payment systems.
